sections.paragraph:
prop. to melt away, to pine, hence to decay, to be brought low, in Kal once, Psa 106:43. (Cognate are מוּג, מוּךְ, מָאַךְ, מָקַק see under מַי page 468, A . Syr. ܡܟܳ to be east down, prostrated.)

Niphal, fut. יִמַּךְ id. to decay (used of a building), Ecc 10:18.

Hophal, pl. הֻמְּכוּ for הֻמַכּוּ they decay, i.e. perish, Job 24:24.
